OpenClaw empowers individuals.
Clawith scales it to frontier organizations.

tools.title: Global Tool Management
tools.initBuiltinsHint: Start the backend first to initialize built-in tools.
tools.emptyState: No data. Start the backend first to initialize built-in tools.
tools.agentInstalledHint: Agent installed tools via `import_mcp_server`. Deleting removes the tool from that agent.
tools.manageGlobalSkills: Manage global skills. Each skill is a folder with a SKILL.md file. Skills selected during agent creation are copied to the agent's workspace.
tools.howToGenerateGithubToken: How to generate a GitHub Token:
tools.githubTokenStep1: 1. Go to github.com → Settings → Developer settings
tools.githubTokenStep4: 4. Set a name and expiration, no scopes needed for public repos
tools.githubTokenStep5: 5. Click "Generate token" and copy the value
tools.githubTokenDesc: Increases GitHub API rate limits from 60/hr to 5,000/hr for skill imports.
tools.clawhubApiKeyDesc: Authenticate ClawHub API calls to avoid rate limiting when browsing and installing skills from ClawHub.
tools.authenticatedRequestsGetHigherRateLimits: Authenticated requests get higher rate limits for ClawHub skill browsing and installation.
tools.searchForSkills: Search for skills on ClawHub marketplace

notificationBar.title: Notification Bar
notificationBar.textPlaceholder: e.g. 🎉 v2.1 released with new features!
notificationBar.description: Display a notification bar at the top of the page, visible to all users.

openclaw.createdDesc2: Your OpenClaw agent has been registered. Copy the instruction below and send it to your OpenClaw agent to complete the setup.
openclaw.setupInstructionDesc: Copy and send this to your OpenClaw agent. It will configure itself automatically.
openclaw.keyNote: This key is already embedded in the instruction above. Save it separately if needed for manual configuration.

slack.step1: Go to api.slack.com/apps → click Create New App → choose From Scratch → enter an app name and pick your workspace → click Create App
slack.step2: In the left sidebar, click OAuth & Permissions → scroll down to Scopes → under Bot Token Scopes click Add an OAuth Scope → add: chat:write, im:history, app_mentions:read
slack.step3: Scroll back to the top of OAuth & Permissions → click Install to Workspace → click Allow → copy the Bot User OAuth Token (starts with xoxb-)
slack.step4: Left sidebar → Basic Information → scroll to App Credentials → click Show next to Signing Secret → copy it
slack.step5: Paste the Bot Token and Signing Secret above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL that appears
slack.step6: Left sidebar → Event Subscriptions → toggle Enable Events ON → paste the Webhook URL into the Request URL field (it will say Verified ✓) → under Subscribe to bot events, click Add Bot User Event and add: app_mention, message.im → click Save Changes
slack.step7: A yellow banner will appear at the top: click reinstall your app and authorize again
slack.step8: Now open a DM with your bot in Slack and send a message to test it
slack.note: Your server must be publicly accessible on the internet so Slack can reach the Webhook URL.

discord.step1: Go to discord.com/developers/applications → click New Application → enter a name → click Create
discord.step2: Left sidebar → Bot → click Reset Token → confirm → copy the Bot Token. Also enable Message Content Intent under Privileged Gateway Intents → Save Changes
discord.step3: Left sidebar → General Information → copy Application ID and Public Key
discord.step4: Left sidebar → OAuth2 → URL Generator → under Scopes check bot and applications.commands → under Bot Permissions check Send Messages and Read Message History → copy the generated URL → paste it in your browser → select your server → Authorize
discord.step5: Fill in Application ID, Bot Token, and Public Key above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL (Interactions Endpoint URL)
discord.step6: Left sidebar → General Information → paste the Webhook URL into the Interactions Endpoint URL field → click Save Changes (Discord will automatically verify the endpoint)
discord.step7: In your Discord server, type /ask message: <your question> to talk to the agent
discord.note: Discord verifies the endpoint immediately when you save, so your server must be running and publicly reachable at that moment.
discord.ws_step1: Go to discord.com/developers/applications → click New Application → enter a name → click Create
discord.ws_step2: Left sidebar → Bot → click Reset Token → copy the Bot Token. Enable Message Content Intent under Privileged Gateway Intents → Save Changes
discord.ws_step3: Left sidebar → OAuth2 → URL Generator → under Scopes check bot → under Bot Permissions check Send Messages and Read Message History → copy the generated URL → paste in browser → select your server → Authorize
discord.ws_step4: Paste the Bot Token above → click Save Config. The bot will connect via Gateway automatically — no public URL needed
discord.ws_note: Gateway mode requires no public IP or callback URL. The bot connects outbound to Discord. Users can @mention or DM the bot to chat.

feishu.step1: Go to open.feishu.cn → log in → click Developer Console (开发者后台) → Create Enterprise Self-Built App (创建企业自建应用) → fill in name and description → Confirm
feishu.step2: In the app page, left sidebar → Add App Capabilities (添加应用能力) → click Add (添加) next to Bot (机器人)
feishu.step3: Left sidebar → Credentials & Basic Info (凭证与基础信息) → copy App ID and App Secret
feishu.step4: Paste App ID and App Secret above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL that appears
feishu.step5: Left sidebar → Events & Callbacks (事件与回调) → Event Config tab → Edit Subscription Mode → select 'Send events to developer server' → paste Webhook URL into the Request URL field → Save (auto-verifies) → click Add Event → search and add im.message.receive_v1 → Save
feishu.step6: Left sidebar → Permissions & Scopes → click 'Bulk Import/Export Permissions' → paste the permission JSON below → confirm import
feishu.step7: Left sidebar → App Release (版本管理与发布) → Create Version → fill in description → click Release for Review → for internal apps, you can approve it yourself in the admin console
feishu.step8: After publishing, invite the bot to a Feishu group or have users add it as a direct contact to start chatting
feishu.note: For internal company apps, the review is usually instant. You must re-publish the app each time you add new permissions.
feishu.ws_step1: Go to open.feishu.cn → log in → Developer Console → Create Enterprise Self-Built App → fill in name and description → Confirm
feishu.ws_step2: Left sidebar → Add App Capabilities → click Add next to Bot
feishu.ws_step3: Left sidebar → Credentials & Basic Info → copy App ID and App Secret
feishu.ws_step4: Select Long Connection (WebSocket) mode above → paste App ID and App Secret → click Save Config
feishu.ws_step5: Left sidebar → Events & Callbacks → Event Configuration → edit Subscription Method → select 'Use Long Connection to receive events' → click Add Event → search and add im.message.receive_v1 (Receive Messages v2.0) → approve required permissions → Save
feishu.ws_step6: Left sidebar → Permissions & Scopes → click Bulk Import/Export Permissions → paste the permission JSON below → confirm import
feishu.ws_step7: Left sidebar → App Release → Create Version → Release for Review → approve it in the admin console
feishu.ws_step8: After publishing, invite the bot to a Feishu group or add it as a direct contact to start chatting
feishu.ws_note: WebSocket mode requires no public IP or callback URL. The connection is managed automatically.

teams.step1: Go to portal.azure.com → Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration → enter name → select 'Accounts in this organizational directory only' (single-tenant) → Register
teams.step2: In the app overview page, copy the Application (client) ID and save it. Then go to Certificates & secrets → New client secret → add description and expiration → copy the Value (this is your App Secret). Also copy your Directory (tenant) ID from the Overview page.
teams.step3: For local testing: Start a localtunnel to expose your backend: `npx --yes localtunnel --port 8000` → copy the tunnel URL (e.g., https://xxxxx.loca.lt). When configuring the Messaging Endpoint URL in Azure Bot, replace 'localhost' with your localtunnel URL.
teams.step4: Paste App ID (Client ID), App Secret (Client Secret), and Tenant ID (Directory ID) above. Click Save Config → copy the Messaging Endpoint URL that appears.
teams.step5: In Azure Bot → Channels → Microsoft Teams → Configure → paste the Messaging Endpoint URL (use your localtunnel URL instead of localhost if testing locally) → Save → the bot will be available in Teams.
teams.note: Your server must be publicly accessible on the internet so Microsoft Teams can reach the Messaging Endpoint URL. For local testing, use localtunnel and replace 'localhost' with your tunnel URL.
teams.tenantIdPlaceholder: Enter your tenant ID (Directory ID) for this single-tenant app
teams.tenantIdHint: Required for single-tenant apps. Get it from Azure AD → App registrations → your app → Overview.

dingtalk.step1: Go to open.dingtalk.com → log in → go to App Development → Create Internal App → enter a name
dingtalk.step2: In the app page → Add Capabilities → find Bot → click Add
dingtalk.step3: In the app info page → copy AppKey and AppSecret
dingtalk.step4: Set message receiving mode to Stream (recommended, no public IP needed)
dingtalk.step5: Paste AppKey and AppSecret above → click Save Config
dingtalk.step6: Publish the app → find the bot in DingTalk and send a test message
dingtalk.note: Stream mode requires no public IP or callback URL. Only AppKey and AppSecret are needed.

wecom.step1: Log in to work.weixin.qq.com → App Management → Self-Built Apps → Create App
wecom.step2: In the app details page → copy CorpID, AgentID, and Secret
wecom.step3: Set up API message receiving → set Token and EncodingAESKey (can be auto-generated)
wecom.step4: Fill in the info above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL that appears
wecom.step5: Paste the Webhook URL into WeCom's API Receive Message URL field → Save
wecom.step6: Find the app in WeCom and send a test message
wecom.note: The callback URL must be publicly accessible. WeCom will verify the URL and send encrypted messages.
wecom.ws_step1: Log in to work.weixin.qq.com → Security & Management → Management Tools → Intelligent Bots
wecom.ws_step2: Click Create Bot → select Manual Creation → choose API Mode at the bottom
wecom.ws_step3: Select 'Long Connection' as the connection method (not URL callback)
wecom.ws_step4: Fill in bot name, avatar, and set visible scope → Save
wecom.ws_step5: Copy the Bot ID and Secret that are generated → paste them above → click Save Config
wecom.ws_step6: Find the bot in WeCom and send a test message
wecom.ws_note: WebSocket mode requires no public IP, callback URL, or domain verification (ICP). The connection is managed automatically.
